framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Designing and teaching a scaled-delivery methodology
paths.what_it_is: Codifying your own delivery practice into a repeatable, teachable methodology (an agile coaching curriculum, a scaling framework, a certification course) and personally training other practitioners and organizations to adopt it.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn tacit delivery instincts into a method other people can actually follow — a structuring skill that scales past any one team you personally run.

paths.what_it_is: Building and personally hosting a recurring, long-running public teaching format — a podcast, interview series, or education site — that explains and demonstrates delivery practice episode after episode for years, not a single viral post.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can sustain a disciplined public habit over years rather than produce one good post — the consistency that builds durable trust.

paths.what_it_is: Writing a regular newsletter or blog, grounded in your own day-to-day program or project work, that turns lived delivery experience into named frameworks and playbooks written for peers doing the same job.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can structure your own experience into something reusable by others — judgment beyond simply delivering your own projects on time.
